initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of left anterior fascicular block?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose left anterior fascicular block, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of right bundle branch block
Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads
Presence of left bundle branch block
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of left bundle branch block

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is left bundle branch block present on this ECG, including incomplete and complete LBBB?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, can this patient be diagnosed with left anterior fascicular block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: Given that left bundle branch block is not present, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated to diagnoses left anterior fascicular block?
options: Presence of a normal QRS axis
Presence of an RSR' pattern in leads V1 and V2

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show left axis deviation, where the QRS axis is between -90 and -30 degrees?

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave that would be used to calculate the QRS axis? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS axis fall into?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this ECG suggest the presence of left anterior fascicular block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the axis deviation, which diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose left anterior fascicular block?
options: Presence of a broad, notched R wave in lead I and aVL
Presence of prominent U waves in leads I and aVL
Presence of inverted T waves in leads I and aVL
Presence of a qR pattern in leads I and aVL
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of a qR pattern in leads I and aVL

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show a qR pattern in any of leads I and aVL?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show the qR pattern? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with qR pattern? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this ECG suggest the presence of left anterior fascicular block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the findings you have identified so far, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose left anterior fascicular block?
options: Presence of an rS pattern in leads II, III, and aVF
Presence of a qR pattern in leads II, III, and aVF
Presence of pathological Q waves in anterior leads
Presence of ST elevation in leads II, III, and aVF
Presence of low voltage QRS complexes in limb leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of an rS pattern in leads II, III, and aVF

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show an rS pattern in any of leads II, III, and aVF?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show the rS pattern? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with rS pattern? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this ECG suggest the presence of left anterior fascicular block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
